test.command: go test ./internal/logging/... && go test -short ./internal/errors/... && go test -short ./internal/output/... && go test -short ./internal/preset/... && go test -short ./pkg/confluence/... && go test -short ./pkg/protheus/...
test.note: Full suite (go test ./...) times out due to integration tests in internal/cli requiring external Protheus SQL Server. Unit tests for logging milestone features pass. Integration tests skipped in this environment.

lint.note: golangci-lint is not installed in this environment. Code review did not find any issues that would be caught by the linter.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The AGENTS.md logging convention specifies 'zap.String("cmd", ...)' but implementation uses 'zap.String("command", cmdPath)'. Align the convention to use 'command' to match implementation.

Add guidance that integration tests in internal/cli require external Protheus SQL Server connectivity. Workers should run unit tests only (go test -short ./...) or package-specific tests when external services are unavailable.
Add step to run logging-specific tests: 'go test ./internal/logging/... -v' when implementing logging features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Workers implementing logging features correctly ran this but it was not documented in skill procedure

rejectedObservations.observation: Backend-worker skill may have transient path resolution issues - worker encountered skill not found but completed successfully
rejectedObservations.reason: Single anecdotal report, no evidence this is a systemic issue
Non-blocking: mapj is a sequential CLI tool, not a concurrent server. No action needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: Two stdout output patterns exist: fmt.Println() vs fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout())
rejectedObservations.reason: Both are valid Go patterns for stdout. No conflict or bug observed. Convention varies by context.

rejectedObservations.observation: NewErrorEnvelopeWithPhase and NewErrorEnvelopeFull are defined but unused
rejectedObservations.reason: Dead code but non-blocking. VAL-LOG-005 criteria do not require phase field to be populated. Could be cleanup suggestion for future.

issues.description: traceID package-level variable is not thread-safe but low risk for sequential CLI
parseLevel silently defaults invalid level strings to info without warning
NewErrorEnvelopeWithPhase defined but never called. Phase field not populated in error responses.
NewErrorEnvelopeFull defined but never used. Dead code.
